description: A commemorative tribute poster from the U.S. Department of Labor's 'Hall of Honor' series, in portrait format with a sepia and aged-photo, grunge texture. The background blends a faded American flag and a muted prairie or landscape under a desaturated brown-and-red palette. In the upper center is a large black-and-white portrait of President Theodore (Teddy) Roosevelt (mustache, pince-nez glasses, dark suit). The header reads 'HALL OF HONOR' in bold cream all-caps with a small red underline beneath 'OF'. A secondary cut-out shows Roosevelt standing outdoors holding papers. A large white quotation-mark glyph introduces the white serif pull-quote: 'A nation’s greatness lies in its possibility of achievement in the present, and nothing helps it more than the consciousness of achievement in the past.' Lower-left credits in white and red read 'PRESIDENT TEDDY ROOSEVELT' and 'INDUCTED SEPTEMBER 29, 2025'. The bottom-right shows the DOL seal and a 'Freedom 250' Betsy-Ross-style 13-star flag mark. The type is gold and cream serif.
notable_text: HALL OF HONOR; 'A nation’s greatness lies in its possibility of achievement in the present, and nothing helps it more than the consciousness of achievement in the past.'; PRESIDENT TEDDY ROOSEVELT - INDUCTED SEPTEMBER 29, 2025; Freedom 250
